title: Learnings-cap lint: warn when .tick/learnings.md exceeds 150 lines
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md phasing row 4. The 150-line cap on .tick/learnings.md is enforced by retro compaction; this lint is the backstop that surfaces drift.

Changes:
1. New helper in a NEW file internal/tick/learnings.go (+ learnings_test.go): CheckLearningsCap(tickDir string) (lines int, over bool, err error) — counts lines of <tickDir>/learnings.md; missing file -> (0, false, nil). Cap constant = 150, exported.
2. cmd/tk/cmd/close.go: after successfully closing an EPIC-type tick, if over, print a one-line warning to stderr: "warning: .tick/learnings.md is <n> lines (cap 150) — compact it at the next retro". Never blocks the close.
3. cmd/tk/cmd/status.go (the tk status command): include the same warning line in its output when over.

Consumer semantics: the warning tells the orchestrator/human that retro compaction has been skipped; it must never fail a command.

Test cases: 151-line file -> over=true; 150 -> false; missing -> (0,false,nil); close of an epic with over-cap file prints warning to stderr and still closes (test at helper level + wire-level if harness allows).

Do NOT touch internal/tick/tick.go or store.go (another tick edits tick.go in this wave — keep files disjoint).

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes incl. helper tests; closing an epic with an over-cap learnings file prints the warning and still closes
closed_reason: Learnings-cap lint merged (helper + close/status wiring, fixture-verified, never blocks)
